texto: Unoka went into an inner room and soon returned with a small wooden disc containing a kola nut, some alligator pepper and a lump of white chalk. "I have kola," he announced when he sat down, and passed the disc over to his guest. "Thank you. He who brings kola brings life. But I think you ought to break it," replied Okoye passing back the disc. "No, it is for you, I think," and they argued like this for a few moments before Unoka accepted the honor of breaking the kola. Okoye, meanwhile, took the lump of chalk, drew some lines on the floor, and then painted his big toe.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chinua Achebe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Unoka went into an inner room and soon returned with a small wooden disc containing a kola nut, s…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, africa, books pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de books e humor.

frase_original: Unoka went into an inner room and soon returned with a small wooden disc containing a kola nut, some alligator pepper and a lump of white chalk. "I have kola," he announced when he sat down, and passed the disc over to his guest. "Thank you. He who brings kola brings life. But I think you ought to break it," replied Okoye passing back the disc. "No, it is for you, I think," and they argued like this for a few moments before Unoka accepted the honor of breaking the kola. Okoye, meanwhile, took the lump of chalk, drew some lines on the floor, and then painted his big toe.

texto: If we have any role at all, I think it’s the role of optimism, not blind or stupid optimism, but the kind which is meaningful, one that is rather close to that notion of the world which is not perfect, but which can be improved. In other words, we don’t just sit and hope that things will work out; we have a role to play to make that come about.

In other words, we don’t just sit and hope that things will work out; we have a role to play to make that come about.

texto: The price a world language must be prepared to pay is submission to many different kinds of use. The African writer should aim to use English in a way that brings out his message best without altering the language to the extent that its value as a medium of international exchange will be lost. He should aim at fashioning out an English which is at once universal and able to carry his peculiar experience.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Chinua Achebe sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “The price a world language must be prepared to pay is submission to many different kinds of use. …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, english, experience, útil em momentos de mudanca e experience.

texto: I feel that there has to be a purpose to what we do. If there was no hope at all, we should just sleep or drink and wait for death. But we don’t want to do that. And why? I think something tells us that we should struggle. We don’t really know why we should struggle, but we do, because we think it’s better than sitting down and waiting for calamity.

texto: In the end I began to understand. There is such a thing as absolute power over narrative. Just as in corrupt, totalitarian regimes, those who exercise power over others can do anything.

texto: Mr. Brown had thought of nothing but numbers. He should have known that the kingdom of God did not depend on large crowds. Our Lord Himself stressed the importance of fewness. Narrow is the way and few the number. To fill the Lord's holy temple with an idolatrous crowd clamoring for signs was a folly of everlasting consequence. Our Lord used the whip only once in His life - to drive the crowd away from His church.

texto: I tell my students, it's not difficult to identify with somebody like yourself, somebody next door who looks like you. What's more difficult is to identify with someone you don't see, who's very far away, who's a different color, who eats a different kind of food. When you begin to do that then literature is really performing its wonders.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chinua Achebe: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. texto: But I liked Yeats! That wild Irishman. I really loved his love of language, his flow. His chaotic ideas seemed to me just the right thing for a poet. Passion! He was always on the right side. He may be wrongheaded, but his heart was always on the right side. He wrote beautiful poetry.

texto: A man who calls his kinsmen to a feast does not do so to save them from starving. They all have food in their own homes.
